title: Modern API development map
summary: The study graph spans REST foundations, Spring implementation, reactive APIs, security, testing, deployment, gRPC, observability, and GraphQL.

title: REST API foundations
summary: REST design uses resources, URI paths, HTTP methods, status codes, HATEOAS, ETag caching, versioning, and stateless security principles.

title: Spring IoC and component model
summary: Spring uses an IoC container, beans, dependency injection, component stereotypes, controllers, services, and repositories to structure API code.

title: OpenAPI design-first implementation
summary: OAS defines API metadata, servers, tags, components, and paths before code generation and Spring implementation work.

title: Business logic and persistence
summary: Business logic is organized behind service and repository components with JPA persistence, database setup, HATEOAS links, and ETag responses.

title: Reactive WebFlux API design
summary: WebFlux and Reactive Streams support asynchronous non-blocking APIs with publisher-subscriber flows, backpressure, Mono/Flux, and reactive persistence boundaries.

title: Spring Security, JWT, and OAuth2
summary: Spring Security protects REST endpoints with filter chains, JWT access tokens, refresh tokens, OAuth2 resource server support, roles, CORS, and CSRF configuration.

title: Testing and deployment
summary: API delivery includes manual and automated testing, unit and integration tests, Docker image packaging, and Kubernetes/minikube deployment.

title: gRPC and observability
summary: gRPC uses Protocol Buffers and separate server/client implementation, while ELK, Zipkin, and Micrometer support logging and distributed tracing.

title: GraphQL development and testing
summary: GraphQL work covers schema design, queries, mutations, subscriptions, N+1 mitigation with DataLoader, instrumentation, and automated tests.
